cards.back: A well-defined procedure that takes some input and produces a corresponding output, using a finite number of steps

cards.front: What is the difference between a recipe and an algorithm?
cards.back: A recipe is a set of instructions for making something, whereas an algorithm is a set of instructions for solving a problem

cards.front: What is a step in an algorithm?
cards.back: A step is a single action or operation that is performed in sequence to solve a problem or achieve a goal

cards.front: What is the purpose of a pseudocode?
cards.back: To provide a high-level representation of an algorithm in a more understandable and readable format

cards.front: What is a time complexity of an algorithm?
cards.back: A measure of the amount of time an algorithm takes to complete, usually expressed as a function of the size of the input
